chapter_context.summary: Aaron begins his priestly ministry with the first offerings for the people.

outline.explanation: This section focuses on Preparation for the First Service (9:1-7), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Leviticus.
This section focuses on Aaron's Personal Offerings (9:8-14), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Leviticus.
This section focuses on Offerings for the People (9:15-21), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Leviticus.

outline.text: The Divine Approval: Fire from Heaven
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Divine Approval: Fire from Heaven (9:22-24), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Leviticus.

key_themes.title: Holiness
key_themes.description: The central call to be set apart as God is holy.

key_themes.title: Atonement
key_themes.description: The necessity of sacrifice to reconcile with God.

devotional.question: How does the detailed nature of these laws reflect God's care for every part of our lives?
In what ways do these ancient sacrifices point us toward the ultimate sacrifice of Christ?

cultural_historical.title: The Sacrificial System
cultural_historical.text: Ancient Near Eastern cultures used sacrifices, but the Levitical system was unique in its focus on moral holiness and direct covenant with one God.
